findings.title: Client-Server Body Mismatch for Baseline Mode
findings.issue: Client sends { baseline_only: true } but server checks body?.mode === 'baseline'. Baseline mode silently runs as regular benchmark for per-case runs.
findings.suggestion: Change client body to { mode: 'baseline' } for both runCase and runAll. Note: comparison/baseline per-case is out of scope per spec - may be a non-issue if baseline per-case is never invoked.
findings.impact: Baseline per-case runs produce skill-augmented results instead of baseline results.

findings.title: Bulk Run History Not Saved During Parallel Execution
findings.issue: runAll fires per-case endpoints without bulk: true in body, so each case writes individual history. The bulk-save endpoint is never called. AC-US4-02 requires a combined bulk history entry.
findings.suggestion: Pass { bulk: true } in body when runAll fires, then call /bulk-save when all cases complete to write the aggregate entry.
findings.impact: History fragmented into single-case entries during parallel runs. Overall pass rate reflects only last case.

findings.title: Spurious Semaphore Release on Missing Eval Case
findings.issue: res.on('close') handler fires release() even when acquire() was never called (early return for missing case). Semaphore guards against underflow but could wake a queued waiter, temporarily exceeding concurrency limit.
findings.suggestion: Set released = true before early returns that happen before sem.acquire().
findings.impact: Could momentarily allow one extra concurrent LLM call.

findings.title: useState Misused for Side Effects in CaseDetail
findings.issue: useState initializer used for side effects (setting prompt/expected text). Only runs on first mount, not when evalCase changes. Stale data when switching cases.
findings.suggestion: Replace with useEffect synced on evalCase.id.
findings.impact: Prompt/expected text areas show stale data from previously selected case.

findings.title: deleteHistoryEntry Path Traversal via Timestamp
findings.issue: Timestamp parameter passed to file operations without strict validation. A crafted timestamp could resolve to paths outside history directory.
findings.suggestion: Validate timestamp matches strict ISO pattern before use in file operations.
findings.impact: Could allow reading/deletion of files outside history directory from malicious localhost pages.
